objective: Create Demo Checkpoint 1 PR 2 as a latency/capacity/cost/access/quota/cache/pre-generation/retention/launch-level contract slice before provider abstraction plus real TTS. Positive claims: C1-CONTRACT-001 latency and first-view performance budgets are explicit; C1-CONTRACT-002 reviewer capacity, cache/pre-generation, and missing-artifact states are explicit; C1-CONTRACT-003 first-month spend target, approval ceiling, quota reservation/refund, retry, timeout, backpressure, and duplicate-spend controls are explicit; C1-CONTRACT-004 retention, deletion, tombstone, provider-side deletion evidence, disclosure, invite/access, and launch-level boundaries are explicit; C1-CONTRACT-005 each changed claim maps to test, gate, source fact, human-only checklist row, or documented non-goal for future PRs. PR2 creates minimum implementation requirements only; it does not approve PR3 provider egress, selected-provider facts, spend, model/voice selection, or dashboard state without fresh PR3 source facts and executable safeguards. Negative invariants: no backend/frontend/provider/RAG/avatar/database/Docker/CI workflow/provider SDK/provider key/runtime implementation; no provider account setup, dashboard configuration, paid plan activation, wallet funding, model or voice selection, real provider test call, or paid spend; no real audio/video generation; no cloned identity; no hosted deployment; no Product Mode 2, public distribution, or production-readiness claim.
